free_text: Measurement of the Thermal Absorption Cross Section
in Lucite Using Fermi Age Theory
Compiled by S.H.
Lucite sample (C5O2H8)n
Data defined as
DATA2= -ln(Cin/Cout)  - transmission ratio R, where
Cin - detector rate when sample in beam
Cout - detector rate with sample out
According to this, the total cross section (DATA1) is
Sig(tot) = -1/(nl) * ln(Cin/Cout)
where
n  - number density of the sample
l  - thickness of the sample.
Data presented in fig. 9 of the reference
sent by author (R.S.)
neutron transmission measurement
performed at the Los Alamos Neutron Science Center.
Lucite sample with chemical composition (C5O2H8)n
No detailed information on sample dimensions given
Absolute statistical uncertainty
Californium source with activity of 825 mu-Ci
Thermal neutron spectrum. It is assumed, that neutrons
from 252Cf source are thermalized in close proximity
of the source and have Maxwellian spectrum in the
lucite bulk sample.
Stack of 18 lucite sheets with dimensions of
35.56 x 35.56 x 1.18 cm3 between two lucite reflectors
with dimensions 35.56 x 35.56 x 6.99 cm3. Overall
dimensions of lucite bulk sample are
Cross section was determined using Fermi Age Theory,
proposed and used by E. Fermi  in in the 1930s. It is
assumed, that the source is monoenergetic and neutrons
are thermalized in a close proximity of the 252Cf
source, which is not completely fullfilled. The
result was corrected for these effects. Neutron flux
density in lucite block was measured at different
distances from 252Cf source by activation of Rh foils
with diameter of 1.26 cm and thickness of 0.03 cm.
Using this method, the number of collisions that
thermal neutrons undergo before being captured is
measured. The number of collisions was determined to be
828 which is the ratio of scattering to absorption
cross section. Scattering cross section was
approximated by neutron total cross section of 433 b,
measured in this experiment at LANSCE.
Eberline HP-260 pancake Geiger-Muller
detector was used to measure activity of Rh foils
No information given
Data taken from text, p. 260 of the reference
